title: quercus coccifera f. tomentosa
intro: Quercus coccifera f. tomentosa, also known as the downy or hairy oak, is a subspecies of the Quercus coccifera species, a type of evergreen oak tree native to the Mediterranean region. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory and astringent, making it useful in treating wounds, reducing fever, and soothing digestive issues. The health benefits of Quercus coccifera f. tomentosa include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and even exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of this plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments and promote overall well-being.
